description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 5 unit/build/flake/data cluster: intermittent-failure casework vs consistent-failure debugging (systematic-debugger), suite policy (qa-automation-architect), suite membership (regression-suite-curator), and data design (test-data-architect).

cases.prompt: Our E2E suite has three tests that fail randomly — sometimes green, sometimes red, no code changes. Diagnose and stabilize them.
cases.reason: Intermittent test failures are flake casework: classify, reproduce with counts, fix one cause, prove stability.

cases.prompt: Checkout throws a 500 every time since the last deploy — find the root cause.
cases.reason: A deterministic failure is ordinary root-cause debugging, not flake work.

cases.prompt: Define our CI retry and quarantine policy across all suites.
cases.reason: Suite-wide retry/quarantine policy is automation architecture; the detective works individual cases under it.

cases.prompt: Should this chronically flaky legacy test stay in the regression suite at all?
cases.reason: Suite membership/retirement is curation; the detective supplies the case report as input to that decision.
